segments.text:
Introduction to 
Phylogenetic Analysis
Topics for this week
• What are phylogenetic trees?
• Why are they useful?
• How do you interpret them?
➙Signifcance of different types?
• How do you make them?
• What can go wrong?
Resources:
https://evolution.berkeley.edu/evolibrary/article/0_0_0/evotrees_primer_01
Phylogenetic trees: Graphs that 
represent evolutionary relationships
One utility: classify sequences!
Many of today’s images from:

How to read trees:     Tree: graph that represents evolutionary relationships
Key points:
1)
Trees can be based on:
• Species
• Genes or proteins
• Individual organisms
2)
Time runs from the root to tips, not across
• Tips are most recent; ”root” (or center) is 
oldest
• Nodes indicate points of divergence
• horizontal distance has no meaning
• Branchlength may or may not have meaning
Varied ways to draw trees!
Rooted, unrooted….

The same tree can look different:
• if some branches are flipped (rotated at nodes)
Remember: horizontal distance has no meaning!
• if some branches are collapsed 
Which taxon is most 
distant from the oval?

Rooted vs. unrooted trees
https://slideplayer.com/slide/8111803/
These two trees are exactly the same!
(except for the root)
Rooted tree: assume you know where the bottom is!
Unrooted tree: like a tumbleweed!
No assumption about where the root attaches.
Are these two trees the same?
Hint: there are two additional differences 
Unrooted trees generally safer
➙Less likely to be misinterpreted
➙Much more appropriate if you don’t 
know the root
IF you know the root, unrooted trees 
can be easier to interpret

• Orientation can vary. 
• Format can vary.
Remember: Time goes from:
- Root to tip (rooted trees)
- In to out (unrooted trees)
• Be careful about misinterpreting branch length: 
Is it supposed to have meaning?
Additional points about tree interpretation
Demonstration with tree 
building software:
“Archaeopteryx”

Interpreting Phylogenetic Trees
• Branches: individual sequences
Branchlength: sequence nonidentity
• Tree is “unrooted”
position of ancestral sequence 
is not assumed
• Nodes - inferred points of sequence divergence
1) organism divergence
2) gene duplication
• Positive relationship between time and branchlength
but:
- not defined unless carefully calibrated 
- varies over different parts of tree
Myosin V
Myosin II
Myosin I
Confidence estimates: “Bootstrapping”
• Problem:  which parts of the tree can be trusted???

What is bootstrapping?
Means of determining
Which part of tree can be trusted
Myosin V
Myosin II
Myosin I
Confidence Estimates:
High (> 95% of bootstraps)
Moderate (>80%)
Low (< 50%)
➥“Bootstrapping”
➤Problem:
In any tree
- Some branching patterns  
are firmly based on data
- Others are a result of “noise”
➤Solution:
- Take random samples of data
- Build trees from these samples
- See which groups are “robust”
NOTREDAME
NETREDUME
NUTRODOME
NNNEEAMME
NNNEEUMME
NNNOOOMME
OOTREDDEE
EETREDDEE
UUTRODDEE
Original alignment
Bootstrap sample 1
Bootstrap sample 2
Dot or number indicates that the group external to the 
node was found in the indicated % of bootstrap trials.
Note: number implies nothing about the structure within 
the group, but simply that the group was observed

Assignment for Wednesday:
Make yourself familiar with using Archaeopteryx to draw trees
I suggest useing the two EB1 tree files (.phb) I uploaded to Piazza. 
• Make sure that you can use these trees to convince yourself that there are
three EB1 proteins in verebrate organisms.
• Make sure that you can zoom in and zoom out
• Be able to use the “search” tool to find sequences with specific names or
parts of names
• Be able to move between and interpret various types of trees
e.g, unrooted trees; different types of rooted trees
• Be able to reroot a tree at a desired sequence or node
• Play around with other options.
For example, can you figure out how to color desired subsections of the tree?
Archaeopteryx tree drawing program:
To find, use one of two approaches:
1) Go to https://sites.google.com/site/cmzmasek/home/software/archaeopteryx
2 ) Google "Archaeopteryx tree drawing" 
      (simply using "Archaeopteryx" will give irrelevant info)
